diagrams.title: Operating System Layered Structure
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing the hierarchical structure of an operating system from hardware at the bottom to user applications at the top, with system programs in the middle layer. This helps visualize where system programs fit in the overall OS architecture.
elements: Hardware layer (bottom) - CPU, Memory, I/O devices
Kernel layer - OS core with process management, memory management, file system
System Programs layer - compilers, editors, file utilities, device drivers
User Applications layer - word processors, games, web browsers
Arrows showing upward/downward data flow between layers
diagrams.purpose: Explains the hierarchical structure of operating systems and the position of system programs as an intermediary between the kernel and user applications

diagrams.title: Categories of System Programs
diagrams.description: A categorized diagram displaying the main types of system programs organized in groups, helping students understand the breadth of system software available.
elements: Central node labeled 'System Programs'
File Management - create, delete, copy, move files
Status Information - date, time, memory usage, disk space
File Modification - text editors, graphics editors
Programming Language Support - compilers, interpreters, debuggers
Program Loading and Execution - loaders, linkers
Communications - email, messaging, remote access
Device Drivers - printer drivers, display drivers
diagrams.purpose: Provides a comprehensive overview of the various categories of system programs and their functions in an operating system

diagrams.title: System Program to Kernel Communication Flow
diagrams.description: A flow diagram showing how user requests travel through system programs to the OS kernel and back, demonstrating the interaction between system programs and the operating system core.
elements: User Application box
System Program box (shell, utilities)
System Call Interface
Kernel box (with sub-components: Process Manager, Memory Manager, I/O Manager, File Manager)
Hardware box
Directional arrows showing request-response cycle
Labels showing system calls (open, read, write, fork, exec)
diagrams.purpose: Explains how system programs act as intermediaries, translating user commands into system calls that the kernel can execute to manage hardware resources
